content.body: annevk: is it right to say WebKit doesn't implement the "active custom element constructor map"?
content.formatted_body: annevk: is it right to say WebKit doesn't implement the "active custom element constructor map"?

content.body: I'm having difficulty understanding this piece of the spec as it seems no one implements it (except Ladybird, who fail the re-entrancy case that I believe this is trying to guard).
keithamus: I'm pretty sure we implement it.
content.formatted_body: keithamus: I'm pretty sure we implement it.

content.body: AFAICT you have `activeCustomElementRegistry` https://searchfox.org/wubkat/source/Source/WebCore/dom/Document.h#590 which is different, it seems. I can't find such a map though.
content.formatted_body: AFAICT you have activeCustomElementRegistry https://searchfox.org/wubkat/source/Source/WebCore/dom/Document.h#590 which is different, it seems. I can't find such a map though.

content.body: Yeah if you implement per spec the re-entrancy tests fail: https://wpt.fyi/results/custom-elements/registries/constructor-reentry-with-different-definition.html?label=master&label=experimental&product=chrome&product=ladybird&product=firefox&product=safari&aligned&view=interop&q=label%3Ainterop-2026-scoped-custom-element-registries
AIUI because HTMLConstructor clears the active constructor map which ofc means re-calling it, it does not have its constructor in the active map.
